meaning: adv.涓嶅彲鑳藉湴,鏃犳硶鍙兂
examples: In midsummer every Cape Cod town is impossibly crowded.
Mathematical physics is an almost impossibly difficult subject.
The Endara government inherited an impossibly difficult situation from its predecessors.
They sat above the ground on stilts, like flamingos balanced on impossibly thin legs.
Bonds yields are impossibly skinny and offer nothing better than parking ones money under the bed.
